claim_text: Benchmark contains 21366 experiments (updated 2026-04-16 from on-disk count)
Benchmark spans 6 function families
Benchmark spans 7 dimensions (1/2/5/10/20/50/100)
Benchmark spans 6 sample sizes (256--8K)
Benchmark spans 5 noise levels (0 to 0.5)
11 training methods constructed from 3 label paradigms x 4 balancing strategies
Warmup(Fuzzy) ranks #1 overall with mean rank 1.8/11
Pathwise methods rank 7--10 on discontinuous payoffs
Fuzzy methods incur <2% value penalty on discontinuous payoffs
Pathwise methods degrade value by 117--562% on discontinuous payoffs
Fixed lambda=1.0 outperforms adaptive balancing in controlled setting
Noise crossover threshold mean 0.085 range [0.03--0.21]
Tier 1 has 1081 experiments
Tier 2 has 6376 experiments
Tier 3 has 9566 experiments
Tier 4 has 721 experiments
Core benchmark total of 17744 experiments across Tiers 1--4
Unified comparison uses 5 discontinuous-payoff datasets
SPY options study has 680 experiments (temporal+CV)
Ablations and diagnostics total 1976 experiments
Warmup(Fuzzy) achieves 1.04x gradient on barrier_bs (NOT 50.2x; regenerated 2026-04-16 from current JSONs)
Warmup(LRM) achieves 6.1x gradient improvement on barrier_bs with +2.2% penalty
Pathwise DML fixed lambda degrades value by +256% on barrier_bs
Kendall tau between value-MSE and gradient-MSE rankings is 0.67
Only Vanilla and Warmup(Fuzzy) are non-dominated on value-vs-gradient Pareto frontier
Fuzzy smoothing is robust to epsilon over 10x range (0.1--1.0)
Warmup value penalty ranges -1.5% to +2.6% across all labels
DML fixed lambda value penalty ranges +0.1% to +256%

DML wins in 112/174 (64.4%) of configs overall (best of fixed/gradnorm/relobralo)
Smooth functions tolerate extreme noise (poly_trig sigma>5)
Trig crosses over near sigma approx 0.5
dml_fixed mean rank 1.0 win rate 39.1% in controlled benchmark
vanilla mean rank 2.0 win rate 24.1%
